sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 13, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 12 of about 61 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The player didn't move at all after the DOWN action. The player is still at rows 24-29. But looking at the ascii, I see the trail pattern at rows 26-27 now:
Row 26: BBBMWMMWwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgRRRRwBBB
Row 27: BBBMWMMWgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwRRRRgBBB
Wait, this is the same as before! The player didn't move. But earlier, after 2 DOWNs, the player moved from rows 18-23 to rows 24-29. Let me re-examine...
Actually, looking more carefully at the ascii output from the previous turn:
After 2 DOWNs:
Row 28: BBBMWWWWMcccccccccccccccccccccccccRRRRcBBB
Row 29: BBBMMMMMMccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccBBB
Now after 1 more DOWN:
Row 26: BBBMWMMWwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgRRRRwBBB
Row 27: BBBMWMMWgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwRRRRgBBB
Row 28: BBBMWWWWMcccccccccccccccccccccccccRRRRcBBB
Row 29: BBBMMMMMMccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccBBB
Wait, the player is still at rows 24-29 according to the bbox. But the trail pattern appears at rows 26-27, which is part of the player's area. This is confusing.
Let me look at the segmentation more carefully. The player bbox is (24, 11, 29, 16), which means the player occupies rows 24-29, cols 11-16. But the trail extends to the right from the player.
Looking at row 26: BBBMWMMWwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgRRRRwBBB
- "BBB" = 3 chars (cols 0-2)
- "MWMMW" = player part (cols 3-7? No wait, the bbox says col 11-16)
Let me recalculate. The ascii string starts at col 0. The bbox says the player is at cols 11-16. So:
- cols 0-2: BBB (3 chars)
- cols 3-10: ??? (8 chars)
- cols 11-16: player (6 chars)
Wait, the ascii I'm printing starts at index 8 (cols 8-50). So:
- Index 0 in my print = col 8 in the grid
- Player at cols 11-16 = indices 3-8 in my print
Row 26: BBBMWMMWwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgRRRRwBBB
- Indices 0-2: BBB (cols 8-10)
- Indices 3-8: MWMMWw (cols 11-16) - this is the player
- Indices 9+: wgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgRRRRwBBB (trail and red target)
So the player is at cols 11-16, rows 24-29. The trail extends from col 17 to the red target at cols 42-45.
Now, the player didn't move after the DOWN action. This is strange. Let me check if the player hit a boundary or obstacle.
Looking at the ascii, the player is at rows 24-29. The green target is at rows 31-34. I need to move the player down 3 more rows to align with green.
But the DOWN action didn't move the player. Maybe there's a boundary or obstacle preventing downward movement. Let me check what's below the player.
Row 30: BBBBBGGBBccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccBBB
Row 31: BBBBBGGBBcccccccccccccccccccccccccbbbbcBBB
The area below the player (rows 30-31) has "BBBBBGGBB" at cols 8-16, which is the left boundary. The player is at cols 11-16, and below it at cols 11-16 is "GGBBcc" (part of the boundary).
Wait, maybe the player can't move down because there's a boundary. Let me check the play area boundaries.
Actually, looking at the image, the play area seems to be bounded by a dark gray region. The player is inside this region. The left side has a gray column, and the right side has the colored targets.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Maybe DOWN doesn't move the player at all, and the earlier movement was something else. Let me re-examine the history.
Actually, I think I made a mistake. Let me re-examine the transitions:
- After UP, UP: player at rows 18-23
- After RIGHT x7: player still at rows 18-23 (RIGHT doesn't move player)
- After DOWN, DOWN: player at rows 24-29
So 2 DOWNs moved the player from rows 18-23 to rows 24-29, which is 6 rows down. That's 3 rows per DOWN.
But then 1 more DOWN didn't move the player. Maybe the player hit a boundary or obstacle. Let me check what's at rows 27-32, cols 11-16.
Row 27: BBBMWMMWgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwwgwRRRRgBBB
- Player at cols 11-16: "MWMMWg" (indices 3-8 in my print)
Wait, that doesn't look right. Let me re-examine.
Actually, I think the issue is that the player is now at a position where DOWN can't move it further. Maybe there's a boundary or the trail is blocking the way.
Let me try a different approach. Instead of moving the player down, maybe I should use MOUSE to click on the green target directly. Or maybe I need to use ACTION7.
Actually, let me reconsider the game. Looking at the HUD at the bottom, it shows: pink, red, green, blue. The red target is now connected (has a trail leading to it). Maybe I need to click on the green target to select it, then extend the trail to it.
Or maybe the game is about drawing a path that visits all targets in order. The trail is the path, and I need to navigate the player to draw the path.
Let me try using MOUSE to click on the green target and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player didn't move after DOWN. Let me check what's blocking.
# Also, let me try MOUSE on the green target to see if it does something.

# First, check the ascii around the player's bottom
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Ascii around player bottom (rows 27-32, cols 8-20):")
for r in range(27, 33):
    print(f"  {r}: {lines[r][8:20]}")

# Try MOUSE on green target (rows 31-34, cols 42-45)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 32, 'col': 43}])

# Check if anything changed
print(f"\nDone: {last_action_result.get('done')}, Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

# Check ascii after MOUSE
lines_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nAscii (rows 30-35, cols 40-48):")
for r in range(30, 36):
    print(f"  {r}: {lines_after[r][40:48]}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Extends trail 2 cols to the right, player stays", "UP": "Moves player up 6 rows", "DOWN": "Moves player down 3 rows, but may be blocked"}, "object_taxonomy": {"player": "Pink (M) 6x6 square with white center", "targets": "red (19-22,42-45), blue (25-28,42-45), green (31-34,42-45)", "trail": "Alternating gray/white blocks"}, "hud_map": {"hud_bar": "Rows 54-63, shows order: pink, red, green, blue"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Trail connected to red. Player at rows 24-29. DOWN didn't move player - possibly blocked. Need to figure out how to reach green target.", "goal_model": "Connect trail to targets in order: red (done), green, blue", "recent_findings": ["DOWN didn't move player at current position"], "open_questions": ["Why didn't DOWN move player?", "Does MOUSE do something?"], "current_plan": "Try MOUSE on green target, then figure out navigation", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
